properties.remark: KFLG ASOS reported visibility below 1/4 a mile in heavy snow.
ADOT weather station at Riordan Overpass reported visibility of 0.08 miles.
Video from social media. A small rotating debris field rips a few shingles off a roof and sends a trash can into the air.
Tribal officials reported trails flooded and impassable along Havasu Creek, with stranded hikers. Structures in the Village of Supai also impacted.
Flash flood in Kanab Creek. USGS Stream Gage on Kanab Creek rose over 6 feet in response to heavy rainfall upstream.

Corrects previous tornado report from 5 NNE Springerville. Large landspout tornado reported by several people on social media, over rural area NE of Springerville. No damage reported at time of report.
Large landspout tornado reported by several people on social media, over rural area NE of Springerville. No damage reported at time of report.
Video and photos sent via email of flooding over N. Reed Road between W 5 N Rd and W 4.5 N Rd from Yuma Wash.
5 foot rise in 45 minutes reported on the Rio de Flag from Coconino County ALERT gauge.
YCSO reports bridge in Spring Valley impassable with water flowing over the road. Car stranded on bridge.
USGS stream gauge on the Little Colorado River near Woodruff reported a 6.3 foot rise in one hour from near nearby thunderstorms producing heavy rainfall.
Video on social media of flash flooding in Big Bug Creek across a low water crossing.
Video on social media of flash flood in Big Bug Creek. Poster says the highest flow since 2021.
Yavapai County river gauge on the Agua Fria River at Kelton Ranch reported a 6.3 foot rise in 15 minutes. Peak flow was 9515 cubic feet per second.
USGS river gauge on the Agua Fria River at Cordes Lakes reported at 5.9 foot rise in 45 minutes from thunderstorms upstream along Big Bug Creek.
Corrects previous hail report from 3 WSW Dewey-Humboldt. Location estimated, size estimated from social media picture.
Rise of 5.01 feet on the Agua Fria River near Humboldt.
Location estimated, size estimated from social media picture.
Corrects location of previous hail report from Cornville. From social media.
From social media. Location estimated.

Low water crossing at N. Contessa Way flooded and impassible with several feet of flowing water. Radar estimated 1.25-1.75 inches occurred in 30 minutes over the Dry Beaver Creek Basin just north of McGuireville.
Social media video of a distant vortex. Formed along intersecting outflow boundaries. Inconclusive whether the rotating column extends to the cloud base. Duration unknown. Damage unknown.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 S Winslow. ADOT reports State Route 87 closed due to flooding of underpass at milepost 342 in Winslow.
USGS stream gauge on Tonto Creek above Gun Creek (TNRA3) reported a 2 foot rise after heavy rainfall upstream earlier this morning.
USGS stream gauge on Cataract Creek below Heather Wash (CCHA3) rose 2.8 feet in an hour due to upstream heavy rainfall.
Report of 2 feet of water in Webber Creek with debris flow from the West Fire scar after heavy rain fell on the scar.
Minor debris flow in gully crossing Control Road (FS 64) after heavy rain fell on the West Fire scar.
Incident reported on AZ511.com of flooding on roadway on I-17 Southbound near Stoneman Lake Rd (MP 306).
DELAYED REPORT: 3.3 foot rise (approx 1050 CFS) recorded on Paria River gauge at 7:30AM MST on July 18.
This is a post-event report from early Monday morning. A USGS stream gage on Jacks Canyon Creek measured a 2.5 foot rise from heavy rain upstream.
The gauge at the Agua Fria River near Humboldt recorded a rise of 5.91 feet.

[DELAYED REPORT] Seven power poles knocked down and/or snapped at 2:54 PM MST over Salt Mine Road from damaging downburst winds. Power knocked out for large area of Camp Verde. Salt Mine Rd blocked for 12+ hours.

Post event report from Sunday evening (8/24). Antelope Canyon Tribal Parks sent photos of water continuing to flow through Antelope Canyon as of 6am Monday morning (8/25). High water marks indicated water depth of 1-2 feet prior to entering Antelope Canyon. Time of report is estimated.
ADOT webcam showed visibility estimated to be near 3/4 of a mile on I-17 near Sunset Point.

Post-event report from Monday night (8/25). A USGS stream gage on Granite Creek (GCRA3) measured a 4.6 foot rise in 30 minutes. A CoCoRaHS observer upstream of the gage confirmed water nearing a business parking lot in downtown Prescott. The observer measured 2.05 inches of rain.
USGS river gauge on the Verde River below Camp Verde (VCVA3) reported a 4 foot rise (1610 cfs) in an hour due to heavy rainfall upstream.
USGS river gauge on the Agua Fria River near Cordes Lakes (AFMA3) reported a 2 foot rise (117 cfs) in 15 minutes due to heavy rainfall along Big Bug Creek.
Yavapai County ALERT gauge on the Agua Fria River at Kelton Ranch reported a 2.7 foot rise in 15 minutes from heavy rainfall in Big Bug Creek.

Post event report from Tuesday (8/26). A USGS stream gage at Lees Ferry measured a 1.2 foot rise on the Paria River at 9:00 PM. Water would have been much higher upstream in the canyon closer to the heavy rainfall. Time is estimated based on radar and the Lees Ferry response downstream.
Facebook post showing video of flooded washes and flooded road. Location is estimated.
Post-event report from 9/3. A downstream stream gauge (TNRA3) measured a 3.02 ft rise in 45 minutes. Can infer flash flooding further upstream near the Rye Creek and Tonto Creek confluence.
Law enforcement reported 3 ft rise on Havasu Creek.
Mesonet station AZT12 Hwy 377 @ 14 Mile Hill MM 376.
ASOS station KSJN St. Johns Airport.
Report from social media.
Social media photo of hail. Size estimated from photo.
Social media photo of hail in Garland Prairie. Size estimated from photo.
Photo from social media showing quarter sized hail in Parks.
Report from trained spotter of quarter size hail at Thorpe Park.
1 Inch hail estimated. Report from Facebook post.
USGS stream gauge on Big Chino Wash near Paulden (BCWA3) reported a 4.1 foot rise (877 cfs) in about an hour and a half.

Reports of Flash Flooding along Havasu Creek at Supai leading to trapped hikers. Upstream gauge (CCTA3) showed a rise from 4.05 ft to 5.95 ft from 1215 to 1245 MST, then another peak of 6.57 ft at 1445 MST. Downstream gauge (HNSA3) shows multiple peaks in streamflow from 800 to 1200 cfs between 1345 and 1800 MST. Time is estimated from upstream stream gauge rise.
County stream gauge at Mayer (54936 - Big Bug Creek @ SR 69) reported a 4.4 ft rise in 30 minutes, peaking at 1:55 pm.
Public report of quarter size hail in Pine.
mPING report of River/Creek overflowing along Sycamore Creek near Pine across FR 64.
Trained spotter reported water rise greater than 4 feet in normally dry Big Bug Creek.
Report of water flowing over Control Road in at least 8 spots west of Geronimo Estates.
Estimated 2 feet of water in Webber Creek flowing over the road entering Geronimo Estates.
Public report of a rapid 4 feet water rise in Pine Creek at Tonto Natural Bridge State Park.
County river gauge (54897 - Agua Fria River blw Ash Creek) measured a 3.75 ft rise from 1700 to 1750 MST with a peak in streamflow at 3354 cfs.
This is a post event report from last Thursday, 10/9. A post on social media shows photos of flash flooding with waterfalls streaming down cliffsides into Lower Antelope Creek.
This is a post-event report from Thursday 10/9. A social media post shows photos of flash flooding with waterfalls streaming down cliff sides into Navajo Canyon near the confluence with Lake Powell.
A USGS stream gage on the East Verde River (EVDA3) reported over a 2 foot rise in 90 minutes.
A USGS stream gage in Jacks Canyon (JCCA3) reported a 5.4 foot rise from widespread rainfall of 0.75 to 1.25 inches upstream.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 6 ENE Lake Montezuma. The Wet Beaver Creek stream gauge near Rim Rock began rising at 2:45 PM MST, and peaked in the Moderate Flooding Category at a gauge height of 11.11 ft at 3:30 PM MST.
The West Clear Creek stream gauge near Camp Verde recorded a rapid 6.5 ft rise.
The West Clear Creek stream gauge near Camp Verde reported a rapid 6.35 ft rise, with the greatest increase in stream height between 3:30 and 4:00 PM MST.
A video on social media showed flash flooding from swiftly flowing water and debris through West Clear Creek made East Verde Lakes Drive impassable beginning at 5:28.
A USGS stream gage on Wet Beaver Creek (WBVA3) upstream of Rimrock recorded a 4.2 foot rise early Saturday morning.
A USGS stream gage on West Clear Creek (WCLA3) upstream of Camp Verde recorded a 3.6 foot rise from widespread rainfall across the basin.
A USGS stream gage on the Moenkopi Wash (MOEA3) measured an 8 foot rise since 9:00 am MST (2 hr and 45 min).
USGS gauge along Moenkopi Wash (MOEA3) reported a 7.2 foot rise over 1.5 hours from heavy rainfall upstream. The wash crest at 26.09 feet (2384 cfs) at 815 PM MST.
A USGS stream gage on the Agua Fria River (AFHA3) near Dewey-Humboldt measured about a 3 foot rise.
Report of flash flooding with a car being swept away along Big Bug Creek near Cordes Lakes. A swift water rescue is ongoing. Location is estimated.
This is a post event report from Wednesday 11/18. Yavapai County Sheriff reported flash flooding with a swift water rescue along Castle Creek near Castle Hot Springs.
This is a post event report from Wednesday, 11/19. A USGS stream gage on Fossil Creek (FCSA3) measured a 3.17 foot rise due to widespread rainfall across the basin.
